page: Amazon Web Services t4g.xlarge is a burstable instance powered by custom 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processors. It features 4 physical cores and 4 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz, with no hyperthreading. The memory configuration consists of 16.0 GB of RAM, providing 4.0 GB per core. This instance operates on the Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and does not include a GPU. Network performance is defined by a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates average performance in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, alongside average large-block memory bandwidth read and write speeds. However, it exhibits weaker performance in memory-intensive and complex computational tasks, with poor ratings in Geekbench single-core and Passmark CPU tests. Cryptographic operations are supported via hardware-accelerated AES, though other advanced vector and matrix extensions are absent.

Positioned as a budget-friendly option, the t4g.xlarge delivers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads with variable CPU utilization. Due to its burstable CPU allocation and moderate memory-to-core ratio, it is best suited for general-purpose applications, development environments, microservices, and low-traffic web serving rather than sustained high-performance computing or large-scale database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services t4g.xlarge is a burstable general-purpose instance powered by a 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor. It features 4 physical cores, 4 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz, and 16.0 GB of RAM, operating on the Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark results show average performance in stress-ng CPU tests, but weaker outcomes in memory bandwidth and single-core Geekbench metrics. With its burstable CPU allocation, the t4g.xlarge offers a cost-efficient profile for workloads with fluctuating resource demands. It is well-suited for development environments, microservices, and low-to-medium traffic web servers.
og_description: Amazon Web Services t4g.xlarge is a burstable ARM64 instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 2.5 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor, designed for cost-effective general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services t4g.xlarge is a burstable ARM64 instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 2.5 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor.
tagline: A burstable ARM64 instance offering balanced compute and memory resources for workloads with variable CPU utilization.
bullet_points: Powered by a 4-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor running at 2.5 GHz
Equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Utilizes a burstable CPU allocation model on the Nitro hypervisor
Lacks local storage and dedicated GPU accelerators
Best suited for development environments, microservices, and light web serving
